title: Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids
content:

'''Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids''' was an underground [[Music/Legends|musical]] group. Named after the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], and [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droids]], the band played on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Adarlon/Legends|Adarlon]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Even though they lacked any real musical talent, they obtained a number of followers among [[Alien/Legends|non-Human]] communities. Although Boba Fett did not show any interest in his namesakes, they attracted the attention of the [[Imperial Board of Culture]], who officially forbade several of their singles in an unsuccessful attempt to stop them from producing music.

==History==
[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and the [[Assassin droid/Legends|Assassin Droids]] was a [[Music/Legends|musical]] group named after Boba Fett, a renowned [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], and assassin [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="G9" /> The band was considered to be newly formed soon after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]],<ref name="G6" /> and were known as the "Galaxy's original talent-free band." They performed a blaring, screechy version of [[Core Drive/Legends|Core Drive]]—a popular and energetic genre of music known for its bass lines mimicking the sound of a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]]. While on tour promoting their first album, Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids clashed with [[Dead Rebels]], who were performing as the opening act for the band. During Dead Rebels' rendition of "[[Death Star Diva]]," their lead singer was knocked unconscious by a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Flute/Legends|flute]]/weapon known as a [[bes'bev]].<ref name="Slugthrowers">{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-part-2|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref>

Throughout the following four [[Galactic Standard Calendar|years]], they obtained a regularly growing community of adherents, and even a cult following. Many of the followers were [[Alien/Legends|non-Humans]], for reasons nobody could explain.<ref name="G9" /> Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids settled on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Adarlon/Legends|Adarlon]], which was far from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Galactic Core]] and, thus, from the most stringent censors of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Droids obtained success and became a fashionable act. Local [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] believed they could make [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] by ferrying audiences to the Droids' concerts.<ref name="G6" />

[[File:InterstellarTransient.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A galactic vagabond wearing a Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids T-shirt in front of [[Graffiti/Legends|graffiti]] of the band's initials]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Droids released several singles to the galactic distribution nets, including "[[IG-88, Where R U?]]"—named after the assassin droid [[IG-88B/Legends|IG-88]]—and "[[If You're a Biped, I Can Make U Limp]]." As their tracks were widespread, they attracted the attention of the [[Imperial Board of Culture]], who considered at least two of the songs, "[[Blow 'em All Away]]" and "[[Who Cares (If Freedom's Gone)?]]," to be anti-Imperial, and thus too dangerous to be broadcast. Those two songs were then banned. The Droids nonetheless were making advances in the [[The galaxy/Legends|intergalactic]] market, and publicly showed their intention to release a complete album in the near future. The band's detractors considered this to be a threat.<ref name="G9" />

Around [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], [[Shirt|T-shirts]] of this band were available for low-income, small-sized humanoids in the planet [[Reuss VIII/Legends|Reuss VIII]], as exemplified by the clothes worn by [[Mygo Skinto]], leader of the [[Rust Rats]] gang of orphaned children, per the description given by [[Shockboxing/Legends|shockboxer]] [[Lobar Aybock]] in [[Fists Of Ion: Memoirs Of A Champion Shockboxer|his autobiography]].<ref name="FOI">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20081031.html|text="Fists of Ion"|int=Fists of Ion|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref>

==Organization==
Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids was a band without any kind of musical talent,<ref name="G6" /><ref name="G9" /> and a style that was far more violent than other underground bands to which they were compared. The group had not attracted the attention of the Mandalorian Boba Fett, and experts believed that he would not deign to take measures against the band if their activities somehow reached his ears.<ref name="G9" />

==Behind the scenes==
Boba Fett and the Assassin Droids were first mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|role-playing]] book ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]] and published in [[1993]] by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="G9" />
